response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a seasoned full-stack engineer with 15+ years of experience, but is fundamentally mismatched for this role on multiple hard requirements. Most critically, the position mandates native-level Japanese language proficiency — a non-negotiable requirement the candidate does not meet, listing only English (fluent), Romanian (fluent), and German (basic). Beyond the language barrier, the role demands deep production-grade C programming for embedded UNIX firmware and Linux kernel investigation and modification, whereas the candidate's primary stack is TypeScript, Node.js, and web technologies. There is no evidence of security domain expertise (MACsec, IEEE 802.1X, Captive Portal) or Layer-2/Layer-3 networking knowledge relevant to LAN-switch firmware development.
strengths: Extensive overall engineering experience (15+ years) spanning web, embedded mentions, and low-latency systems at Sociomantic
Demonstrated C/C++ exposure at National Instruments and in personal projects, indicating baseline systems programming awareness
Strong engineering discipline: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and open-source contributions reflect professional maturity
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency — a listed hard requirement; candidate's languages are English, Romanian, and basic German only
Primary production expertise is TypeScript/Node.js/web; no evidence of embedded firmware C development or Linux kernel source modification
Zero demonstrated knowledge of required security or networking technologies: MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal, L2/L3 switching, or chip-vendor SDKs

key_factors.factor: No embedded C / Linux kernel expertise
No security or networking domain knowledge
